text:
Paris Olympicsathletes have reportedly been granted free access to one of Paris’s top-rated adult venues during the Games. Thousands of sporting heroes have travelled to France for the Olympics this month, with all events beginning today (Saturday).

Athletes looking for some fun away from their sports during their stay in the French capital can use their accreditation to gain free access to aduly entertainment venue Secret Square. The venue claims it wants to prioritise those representing their nations this month.

The offer, which is also open to members of the media who are covering the Games, might be tempting for athletes looking to get away from the Olympic Village. It is only a 12-mile drive from the athletes’ base.

"During their stay in Paris, athletes, their team and journalists covering the event won't have to pay the entrance fee," a spokesperson allegedly toldTMZ.

The strip club is reportedly telling athletes and journalists to simply show their Olympics accreditation pass for free access. The venue is famous for displaying “French striptease”.

Secret Square has also put on a sport-themed night to lure athletes inside. Dancers wore uniforms while performing on the pole and stage as the opening ceremony was shown on a screen.

The Paris Olympics has once again been rife with rumours of athletes sleeping in ‘anti-sex’ beds. The myth first arose at the 2021 Games in Tokyo.

Photos of beds went viral three years ago, with it incorrectly believed that the cardboard structure was to deter athletes from having sex with each other. However,many individuals staying at the Olympic Village have posted videosshowing that the beds are perfectly durable.

Pick up your ultimate guide to the Olympics! Going for Gold provides the day-by-day rundown of what's on and when. Clickhereto buy.



There is concern about the comfort of the beds, though. Athletes will be desperate to experience good sleep in Paris to ensure they are in top condition for their events, with many expecting to travel home with medals. A lack of sleep may prevent them from performing to the best of their abilities.

Meanwhile, Saturday saw the start of all the main events at the Olympics. Some sports, including football and handball, had already begun earlier this week.Team GB have secured two medalson Saturday.

Scarlett Mew Jensen and Yasmin Harper winning bronze in the women’s synchronised 3m springboard. Cyclist Anna Henderson then took advantage of some chaos in the women’s time trial to snatch Great Britain’s first silver medal on a successful opening day.

title: Olympic athletes and journalists granted free strip club access as Paris offer emerges | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Sam Smith
description: Olympic athletes reportedly have free access to a high-profile Parisian strip club.
